allies.rationale_text: Root-cause-analysis is the closest ally of five-whys-method because the why-chain is a practical route from proximate symptom to deeper mechanism.
allies.source_quote: The Five Whys is a core method for establishing the difference between proximate cause and root cause.

allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking benefits from five-whys-method because repeated questioning strips away assumptions until only the operative facts remain.
allies.source_quote: The Five Whys serves as the practical tool for executing the philosophical mandate of First Principles.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking keeps five-whys-method honest by showing when the problem has interaction effects or parallel causes that a single why-chain would miss.
allies.source_quote: The analyst may stop at five steps and miss critical variables or interconnected forces.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias distorts five-whys-method when the questioning is steered toward the story the investigator already wants to prove.
antagonists.source_quote: five-whys-method conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when confirmation Bias can hinder the Five Whys Method by leading to biased questioning and conclusions.

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring corrupts five-whys-method when the first answer fixes the chain and all later questions orbit that initial suspect.
antagonists.source_quote: five-whys-method conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when anchoring may conflict with the Five Whys Method by causing initial answers to overly influence subsequent questions.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Five-whys-method conflicts with confirmation-bias when biased questioning steers the chain toward a preferred conclusion.
structured_tensions.source_quote: five-whys-method vs confirmation-bias: five-whys-method conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when confirmation Bias can hinder the Five Whys Method by leading to biased questioning and conclusions.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Five-whys-method conflicts with anchoring when the initial answer overly influences every later question in the chain.
structured_tensions.source_quote: five-whys-method vs anchoring: five-whys-method conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when anchoring may conflict with the Five Whys Method by causing initial answers to overly influence subsequent questions.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on root-cause and first-principles allies plus two explicit tensions against biased or anchored questioning.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor troubleshooting relation sprawl beyond the strongest diagnostic allies and explicit anti-bias tensions.
open_questions: Critical-thinking and iteration are source-real neighbors, but root-cause-analysis, first-principles-thinking, and systems-thinking were the cleaner bounded anchors for this pass.
